core_mechanism: a tight empirical power-law scaling relation between two directly-measured observables, calibrated (anchored) on a reference subsample with independently known values, then used as a proxy/standard-ruler estimator to infer an otherwise-unmeasurable quantity across the whole sample and constrain the parameters of a global model
brief_reason: The method rests on the L(Hβ)-σ relation calibrated on local anchor systems with primary distance measurements and extended across the full sample as an independent distance estimator from which model parameters are then inferred.
